steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence has one main verb: 'was thereby empowered.' Whatever fills the blank must not create a second main clause, or the result is a comma splice. The blank introduces the subject 'Pennsylvania' along with information about when it ratified the Constitution. A participial phrase can attach that information to Pennsylvania without adding a second finite verb.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Pennsylvania would officially become: 'would become' is a finite verb, creating an independent clause followed by a comma and another independent clause ('was thereby empowered') — a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice B — Pennsylvania had officially become: 'had become' is also a finite verb, producing the same comma splice. ✗
  • Choice C — Pennsylvania officially became: 'became' is a finite verb — same comma splice error. ✗
  • Choice D — Pennsylvania, having officially become: 'having officially become' is a participial phrase modifying Pennsylvania, not a finite verb. It leaves 'was thereby empowered' as the sentence's only main verb — grammatically correct. ✓
D is correct.
